title: Analytics & instrumentation
read-when: Specifying or implementing how usage, outcomes, and arc-conformance are measured.

title: Recall substrate (gbrain)
read-when: Specifying or implementing the gbrain recall integration — config, reads/writes, compatibility.

title: Concepts
read-when: You need the shared mental model before reading any spec.

title: Decomposition principles
read-when: Deciding how to break an arc into nodes, edges, and inline elements.

title: DevOps — how stack-graph develops itself
read-when: Working on stack-graph itself — branching, PRs, or the improvement loops.

title: Graph specification
read-when: Defining or implementing the node/edge model, the node schema, or storage.

title: Harness specification
read-when: Defining how a consuming workspace customises the vendored graph.

title: Directory topology
read-when: Laying out a consuming workspace on disk — where the vendored graph, the overlay, the canon, working files, and generated data live.

title: Maintenance skill (sg-graph-maintainer)
read-when: Specifying or building the skill that authors and maintains the graph.

title: stack-graph handbook
read-when: Orienting to stack-graph — what it is and how this handbook is organised.

title: Writing node instructions
read-when: Authoring or amending a node body — the skill or agent instructions a node renders into.

title: Authoring handbook pages
read-when: Writing or editing a stack-graph handbook page.

title: Maintenance — the forcing rule
read-when: You hit drift, ambiguity, or a missing page while working in stack-graph.

title: Plugin specification
read-when: Packaging the plugin, building the handbook→plugin pipeline, or understanding how the handbook is rendered.
